<!--
ConsensusBot issue template.

Use this structure for bugs, enhancements, and refactors. Sections in
square brackets are optional — drop them if they don't apply. Don't
rename sections; downstream tooling and agents key off these headings.

Style: concise, evidence-first. Link to file:line, SPEC §, or commit
SHA rather than describing things in prose. Prefer one-line bullets
over paragraphs. The Acceptance list is the contract — write it so
"done" is unambiguous.
-->

## Problem

<!-- One or two sentences. What is wrong / missing / confusing? -->

## Repro / Background

<!--
For bugs: exact steps to reproduce, the failure output verbatim
(in a fenced block), and the environment (`slack run` vs `slack deploy`,
branch, OS, etc.).

For enhancements: the user-facing situation that motivated this — a
screenshot, a quote, the SPEC section, or a link to the discussion.

Cite file:line for any code referenced.
-->

## Why this matters

<!--
Impact and blast radius. Who is affected, how often, and what does it
block? If the discovery cost was disproportionate to the fix cost,
say so — that's a signal worth capturing.
-->

## Proposed approach

<!--
Pick one path, or rank options (a / b / c) with the trade-offs. Cite
the SPEC section that anchors the change. If multiple options are
viable, state which is recommended and why. For pure-bug issues a
single approach is usually enough.
-->

## Acceptance

<!--
Checkbox list of conditions that mean "done". Each item should be
independently verifiable. Include test coverage as an item when the
fix has regression risk.
-->

- [ ]
- [ ]

## Context

<!--
Where this surfaced (PR #, branch, conversation), related issues
(#NN), and any links the next reader will want. Keep to two or three
lines.
-->
